slides.title: What is a Set?
slides.text:
A Set is just a collection of distinct objects.

Imagine a basket. Anything we put inside that basket is a 'member' of the set.

slides.title: 1. Finite Sets
slides.text:
A Finite Set has a specific number of members. You can count them, and the counting comes to an end.

Example: Vowels = {a, e, i, o, u}

slides.title: Cardinal Number
slides.text:
Because a Finite set ends, we can find its Cardinal Number, n(A).

This is just the total count of members.

slides.title: 2. Infinite Sets
slides.text:
An Infinite Set has no end. It goes on forever!

Imagine counting numbers: 1, 2, 3, 4... You could never finish.

slides.title: The Ellipsis (...)
slides.text:
Since we can't write all the numbers, we use three dots called an Ellipsis.

{ 1, 2, 3, ... }

This symbol means 'and so on forever'.

slides.title: Summary
slides.text:
• Finite: Ends. Can be counted.
  • Infinite: Never ends. Uses '...'
